name: Oryol Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the courage of Oryol's people and the colours of the Russian Empire
the Oka River and the sky

description: Two horizontal stripes of red over light-blue in proportions 4:1; the coat of arms of Oryol Oblast is placed in the centre of the red stripe.
history: Adopted on 31 July 2002 by the Council of People's Deputies of Oryol Oblast.

features.symbolism: red recalls the courage and heroism of the Oryol people during the Great Patriotic War; the light-blue band represents the Oka River.

features.role: coat of arms of Oryol Oblast
features.symbolism: a blue shield bearing a silver city gate surmounted by a black eagle (the canting symbol of Oryol — Russian for 'eagle'), ensigned by the imperial crown.
